title: Define application/bolyra-proof+cbor content type and envelope format

artifacts.description: CDDL schema defining the bolyra-proof envelope: proof bytes, public signals array, circuit identifier, proving system tag, and protocol version
ABNF grammar for HTTP Content-Type and Accept header negotiation for application/bolyra-proof+cbor
IETF-style draft specifying the media type registration template (per RFC 6838), envelope semantics, versioning policy, and interop requirements
Reference encoder/decoder: serializes ProofResult to CBOR envelope and parses incoming envelopes with schema validation
Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode for groth16 and plonk proof types, version field handling, malformed input rejection
Canonical test vectors (hex-encoded CBOR blobs) for each circuit/proving-system combination, used by conformance runner
Developer-facing guide: how to set Content-Type, consume the envelope in middleware, migrate from raw proof bytes

steps: Audit existing SDK ProofResult shape and HTTP headers used in examples/mcp-demo to identify all fields currently exchanged ad-hoc

Draft CDDL schema: define bolyra-proof-envelope as a CBOR map with required keys: version (uint), circuit-id (tstr), proving-system (tstr enum groth16/plonk), public-signals (array of bstr), proof (bstr), and optional metadata map
Write ABNF for Content-Type parameter negotiation: circuit-id and proving-system as optional type parameters (e.g. application/bolyra-proof+cbor; circuit=AgentPolicy; ps=groth16)
Compose IETF-style media type registration draft following RFC 6838 section 4.2: type name, subtype, required parameters, encoding considerations, security considerations, interoperability notes
Implement encode(proof: ProofResult): Uint8Array in sdk/src/envelope.ts using cbor-x (already a transitive dep via snarkjs); map ProofResult fields to CDDL-defined keys
Implement decode(buf: Uint8Array): ProofResult with CDDL-conformant validation: reject unknown proving-system values, enforce version semver range, verify public-signals length matches circuit arity
Generate canonical test vectors for HumanUniqueness/groth16, AgentPolicy/groth16, AgentPolicy/plonk, Delegation/groth16, Delegation/plonk using the fast (mock) proof path
Write unit tests covering: round-trip fidelity, unknown version rejection, wrong circuit-id detection, truncated CBOR rejection
Integrate envelope encode/decode into verifyHandshake() and expose encodeProofEnvelope()/decodeProofEnvelope() as public SDK API
Update bolyra-proxy.js MCP server to set Content-Type: application/bolyra-proof+cbor on proof responses
Write docs/wire-format.md with curl examples, middleware routing snippet, and migration notes for consumers currently using raw proof bytes
Run conformance runner against test vectors to confirm spec and implementation agree before marking complete
dependencies: cbor-x (transitive via snarkjs — verify it is directly importable in sdk/)
RFC 9165 or CDDL toolchain (cddl npm package or cddl-codegen) for schema validation in tests
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md (IETF draft to reference/link from the new envelope draft)
circuits/build/ zkey artifacts (needed to generate conformance test vectors via fast proof path)
